title: cleistopholis gracilipes
intro: Cleistopholis gracilipes, also known as the African starfruit or starfruit tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a valuable resource in herbal medicine. Cleistopholis gracilipes is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies for its antiseptic and antiviral properties.
